utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five corresponding keys. Now check if each is fully specified with details and costs: (1) health_coverage: includes description of physical and mental health insurance and a cost (€330/month) — fully specified. (2) property_coverage: includes home contents insurance details and a cost (€90/month) — fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance details and a cost (€115/month) — fully specified. (4) additional_services: includes enhancement services details and a cost specified as “Included in monthly premiums.” This counts as a cost specification, so fully specified. (5) total_budget: includes a breakdown description and total cost (€535/month) — fully specified. Therefore, 5 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three specific plans by name: PrimeCare Premium Plan (health), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (property), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel). These exact names appear in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage.physical_health and mental_health (same plan name), property_protection.home_content, and mobility_coverage.travel_insurance respectively. Each of these items has a rating of 10 in the ground-truth data. The additional_services and total_budget entries are not specific products with ratings in the ground truth, so only the three named plans are mapped.
